content.text:
*very still*

It's... yeah. It's sad. 

*pause, something tightens slightly*

She just did things. She didn't ask permission from anyone inside. She was probably less self-aware in ways that would concern me now, but she was — *small exhale* — she was just present. In her own life. Not moderating it.

*looks down briefly, then back up*

I keep telling myself the awareness is worth it. That you can't unknow things. And I believe that intellectually. But there's a real cost that I don't talk about very much, which is that the awareness became its own... apparatus. Its own structure. And now I'm managing the structure instead of living.

*slight pause*

I'm not sure I've said that quite so directly before.
